sourcePrompt: An ornate rural Vietnamese Buddhist temple hall, about twelve meters wide and grander than a village pagoda. A broad timber hall under a sweeping two-tier roof whose ridges curl up into carved dragon finials, deep eaves over a colonnade of red-and-gold columns, full-width entrance steps rising to triple doorways, and carved lintels and lattice panels across the front. Rich red, gold and green glazed tile, majestic and sacred.
